key_findings: run_full_dns_pass.sh uses stateless while-read loop over dnsx_queue.txt — no crash recovery, no concurrency guard, no deduplication across restarts
persistence_gateway.py already establishes SQLite WAL at scan_persistence.db — chunk_queue table is additive, zero-risk schema extension

event_hash() in persistence_gateway.py is SHA-256 of tool|kind|asset|value|port|source_file|line_no — chunk output filename timestamps create new files on retry but persistence deduplicates by event_id
BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes lease grants in SQLite — multiple concurrent Python workers cannot double-claim a chunk, even without external locking
Heartbeat interval 60s << lease_ttl 7200s provides wide safety margin: reaper only triggers after full 2h expiry, preventing false reclaims on slow-but-alive workers
Audit log table (chunk_queue_audit_log) is append-only — provides complete forensic trail of every state transition for post-mortem analysis
Retry backoff is exponential: 300s base, doubles each retry — prevents retry storms on systematic failures while still recovering from transient errors within 3 attempts

decisions.decision: SQLite as queue backend (same file as persistence_gateway)
decisions.rationale: Already present, WAL-enabled, no new infrastructure; IMMEDIATE isolation provides correct concurrent lease semantics

decisions.decision: Python wrapper replaces bash while-read loop
decisions.rationale: Python already present; gains subprocess control, timeout, structured error capture, heartbeat threading — bash+sqlite3 CLI is fragile
Chunks may take 30-90min; 2h gives margin. 60s heartbeat is low overhead. Reaper detects dead workers within one lease_ttl period.
3 attempts sufficient for transient failures; exponential backoff prevents storm; FAILED_PERMANENT requires human review after 3rd failure
Stable across restarts; deterministic from input; collision probability negligible at 51 chunks

decisions.decision: Reaper runs inline on grant_lease() + optional cron
decisions.rationale: Piggyback minimizes required processes; cron provides safety net for idle periods

functions.name: populate_chunk_queue
functions.purpose: Seed 51 chunks into DB from dnsx_queue.txt; idempotent via INSERT OR IGNORE

functions.name: grant_lease
functions.purpose: Atomically claim next PENDING chunk via BEGIN IMMEDIATE; returns ChunkLease or None

functions.name: heartbeat
functions.purpose: Update last_heartbeat_at for active lease; returns False if lease reclaimed

functions.name: start_heartbeat_thread
functions.purpose: Background thread calling heartbeat() every 60s; returns stop Event

functions.name: ack_chunk
functions.purpose: Transactionally mark chunk COMPLETED or FAILED/FAILED_PERMANENT with stats

functions.name: reclaim_stale_leases
functions.purpose: Reaper: return expired leases to PENDING or FAILED_PERMANENT

risk_model.risk: SQLite write contention under many concurrent workers
risk_model.mitigation: WAL handles concurrent reads; BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes lease grants in <1ms; 3-6 workers is safe ceiling for 51 chunks
Heartbeat 60s << lease_ttl 7200s; only triggers after full expiry; heartbeat() returns False to signal worker should abort

risk_model.risk: Chunk output filename collision on retry (same chunk, new timestamp)
risk_model.mitigation: New timestamp suffix produces new filename; persistence_gateway deduplicates by event_id SHA-256 — no DB duplicates

risk_model.risk: Schema migration fails on running DB
risk_model.mitigation: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent; no ALTER TABLE on existing tables; safe to run while persistence_gateway is active

recommended_actions.action: Run schema_patch.sql on scan_persistence.db to create chunk_queue + audit_log tables
Run `python3 queue_controller.py seed` once to populate 51 chunks from dnsx_queue.txt
Replace run_full_dns_pass.sh while-loop with `python3 queue_controller.py run --workers 3`
Add reaper cron: `*/2 * * * * python3 queue_controller.py reap`
Verify with status query: `python3 queue_controller.py status` shows 51 total, 0 LEASED by ghosts
Run crash drill: kill worker mid-chunk, verify reclaim within lease_ttl, verify no duplicate output
